title: Vampirism Integrations
description: Addon to Vampirism providing more mod integrations
body:
![Banner](https://i.ibb.co/ZdcqfFD/vampirism-integrations-title.png) This is an addon to the mod [Vampirism](https://minecraft.curseforge.com/projects/vampirism-become-a-vampire) (therefore Vampirism is required). ### About: This mod handles integrations or respectively compatibility with other mods. While the JEI and GuideAPI integration will stay inside the main mod, any additional compatibility will be implemented here. Therefore, this mod is only useful if you are using one or more of the following mods: ### Current Integrations (not always up-to-date): **HWYLA/WAILA/WTHIT/Jade** - See blood levels, garlic info and creature status **Minecraft Comes Alive**/**MCA Reborn** - Enables players to suck blood from MCA villagers - Adds a converted (vampire) version of MCA's villager. - Tries to suck blood from other villagers/creatures at night - Avoids sun - Adds angry version of MCA's villager - Normal villagers become angry if too many villagers are bitten - Attacks villagers - General village compatibility improvements **Consecration (1.16-1.5)** - Add vampires as undead creatures - Add holy water as holy/smite damage **Biomes O' Plenty** - No sun damage in biome "Omnious woods" and others **Abysalcraft (1.12)** - No sun damage in biome "Darklands" **Blood Magic** (1.12, 1.16) / **Evilcraft** / **Tinkers Construct** (1.12) - Convert the respective blood to Vampirism's blood by filtering it using the blood sieve **Toroquest** (1.12) - Guards and sentrys attack vampires **Tough As Nails** (1.12) - Temperature related things **Survive** / **ColdSweat** - Grant vampires cold resistance, but decrease heat resistance - No thirst **Crafttweaker** - Allow editing alchemical cauldron and weapon table recipes **ChoiceTheoremOverhauledVillage** - Village building compatibility **GuardVillager** - Respect village faction ### FAQ ##### Why is this a separate mod?
  1. Mod integrations are a lot of work regarding Minecraft updates. If MC updates, but the other mods are not finished yet, I have to uncomment all the relevant parts in Vampirism codes, remove the dependencies and redo all these things once the mod eventually updates.
  2. If a other mods changes and requires changes, I don't want to release an update for Vampirism each time
  3. It keeps Vampirism's code slightly more organized
  4. It verifies that Vampirism's API is working, shows me areas for impovement and can act as an example.
  5. It makes it more straight-forward for others contribute and add more integrations (hope this is gonna happen, but I did not have any luck so far)

The most important integrations will stay in Vampirism (JEI, GuideAPI,?), but any additional (hopefully many) will be added into the seperate mod.
If you are already installing many mods or a modpack it should not make that much a difference anyway.

#####  Can you add more integrations? Feel free to suggest integrations for any other mod (on the Vampirism Forum [thread](http://www.minecraftforum.net/forums/mapping-and-modding-java-edition/minecraft-mods/2756555-vampirism-become-a-vampire-500k-downloads) or as an issue on [Github](https://github.com/TeamLapen/VampirismIntegrations/issues)). But please be specific about what exactly you want to see. ##### Modpacks? Yes, but give credit and link this page ### Installation 1. Install the minimum required version of [Vampirism](https://minecraft.curseforge.com/projects/vampirism-become-a-vampire) (usually the recommend release). Minecraft/Forge will tell you on startup if you are using an out-of-date version 2. Drop this in your mods folder
